mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust manufacturing activity, which correlates with higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of rising INDPRO are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC falls below 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.0% indicates tightening labor markets, which drives wage growth and corporate profit margins, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods of UNRATE < 4.0% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal fails to outperform baseline in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL signal aggressive monetary tightening amid high inflation, which raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in scenarios with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 320 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction effect collapses in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS above 4.5% during a monetary tightening cycle compresses credit availability, reducing corporate investment and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in FEDFUNDS > 4.5% regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) reflects strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in GDP growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the relationship breaks down during recessions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.25 million units signals strong consumer confidence and construction activity, which boosts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in HOUST > 1250 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal fails to outperform benchmarks in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 150,000 new jobs indicates weakening labor market conditions, which lowers consumer spending and generates negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in PAYEMS < 150000 periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in RSAFS (consumer credit) and HOUST (housing starts) signals reduced consumer and business spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days due to weakened demand dynamics.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in scenarios with RSAFS < 715000 and HOUST < 1250 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the interaction effect is not statistically significant.
